ip_outcome_note: Card name matches species name. Art depicts a small brownish creature wearing a white skull as a helmet — canonical Cubone design. PokemonTCG.io confirms card name 'Cubone', National Pokedex no. 104, Unified Minds (sm11-105). Identity confirmed.

trivia_bullets.text: Cubone wears the skull of its dead mother as a helmet — one of the franchise's most cited examples of dark design. In the original Red and Blue, the backstory is made concrete in Lavender Town: a Marowak ghost haunts the Pokemon Tower because she was killed by Team Rocket while protecting her Cubone child; that specific ghost is Cubone's mother, and she prevents the player from ascending to the tower's top floor until the Silph Scope is obtained [Bulbapedia: Marowak (ghost), via WebSearch snippet].
Flavor text on this card (confirmed via PokemonTCG.io): 'At night, it weeps loudly for its dead mother, but those cries only attract its natural enemy — Mandibuzz.' [PokemonTCG.io sm11-105]

National Pokedex no. 104. Cubone is a Ground-type from Generation I whose design has remained unchanged across every generation — the skull-helmet and bone weapon are its defining identifiers with no alternate forms. Artist for this printing is otumami [PokemonTCG.io sm11-105].

In Pokemon Let's Go Pikachu and Let's Go Eevee, the Lavender Town subplot is expanded: the player helps reunite the Cubone with its mother's ghost, after which Marowak's spirit departs peacefully without requiring a battle — a direct narrative resolution to the Gen I dark event [Bulbapedia: Marowak (ghost), via WebSearch snippet].

related_cards.note: Cubone's evolution — and canonically the species of the mother whose skull Cubone wears

related_cards.name: Mandibuzz
related_cards.note: Cubone's natural enemy named in the flavor text on this card
